summary: High‑energy second set with scorching Bertha and stellar Dylan trio
blurb: The show bursts with a tight rhythm section, Jerry’s pedal‑steel on “I Want You” and “Baby Blue,” and a blistering second set featuring The Deal → Bertha → Sugar Magnolia. The third‑set Dylan set adds emotional depth while the band stays locked in throughout.
review: July 24, 1987 at Oakland‑Alameda County Stadium captures the Dead at full throttle. Set one opens with a crisp “Jack Straw” and rolls through “Mississippi Half-Step Uptown Toodeloo,” but it’s the second set that really shines – a fiery “Deal” into a soaring “Bertha → Sugar Magnolia” transition, a punchy “Scarlet Begonias,” and an unrelenting jam that keeps the stadium moving. Jerry Garcia adds unexpected color with pedal‑steel on “I Want You,” “It’s All Over Now Baby Blue,” and the Dylan‑centric third set, while his crystal‑clear tone carries soaring solos throughout. Phil Lesh’s heavy bass lays down a solid foundation, especially on “Highway 61 Revisited,” and Brent Mydland delivers smashing keyboards, eerie undertones, and a memorable “Far From Me” insertion. Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann drive the groove with tight, energetic drumming that propels every set. Though the Dylan set has a few vocal slips (“Touch of Grey” lyric flub) and “Baby Blue” is noted as slightly sloppy by some listeners, the overall performance is vibrant, well‑grooved, and memorable – a high‑octane Dead show captured in excellent soundboard quality.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across five recordings, two are clearly positive, two mixed and one negative. The majority of comments praise the band's tight playing, standout songs (Deal, Bertha, Scarlet Begonias) and Jerry’s pedal‑steel, with only minor criticisms about a lyric flub and occasional sloppy moments. This yields a solid positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard capture with crisp, clear mix; no dropouts and highlights Jerry’s pedal‑steel on the Dylan set.

key_highlights: Second set fires with Deal → Bertha → Sugar Magnolia and a standout Scarlet Begonias.
Jerry’s pedal‑steel adds unique texture to I Want You, Baby Blue and the Dylan third set.
Tight rhythm section: Phil’s heavy bass drives Highway 61; Mickey & Bill keep the groove relentless.
Soundboard recordings are superbly clear, offering a full stadium experience.
song_highlights: It's All Over Now Baby Blue
Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again

band_performance.Jerry: Pedal‑steel and slide work shine, delivering a treat on I Want You, Baby Blue, and the Dylan set; his tone is crystal clear and solos on Bertha and Ballad of a Thin Man are soaring.
band_performance.Phil: Heavy, driving bass that powers the groove, especially evident on Highway 61 Revisited and throughout the high‑energy jams.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm guitar work and dependable vocals that support Jerry’s leads, keeping the band grounded during the marathon sets.
band_performance.Brent: Smashing keyboard solos with eerie undertones; memorable Far From Me insertion and fills admirably on Baby Blue outro.
band_performance.Mickey: Tight, energetic drumming that propels each set forward, locking in with Bill for a powerful dual‑drum foundation.
band_performance.Billy: Dynamic drum work that complements Mickey, maintaining relentless momentum throughout the show.

must_listen_sequences: It's All Over Now Baby Blue
Stuck Inside of Mobile with the Memphis Blues Again
